tool: Siril 1.4.4 — 373 unregistered lights: CFA convert -> median stack (-norm=mul); Siril regional stat + autostretch preview
purpose: sky-flat viability test for flatless set-01 (real flats impossible: lens cleaned post-session). Empirical gate on IFN contamination.

finals_comparison.run: 124 frames (every 3rd), master-dark calibrated, CFA, framing=min crop; proper flat = median of DARK-SUBTRACTED lights (pedestal-free — the raw sky flat's pedestal would under-correct); with vs without sky flat, registered (2-pass) + rej-stacked
finals_comparison.result: WIN. The sky flat corrects the illumination (0.44 -> 1.06; the central vignetting glow removed) AND preserves the cosmic dust: the dark-nebula / Milky-Way dust lanes are REVEALED (buried under the vignetting glow without the flat), not erased. Dust-preservation gate PASSES.
finals_comparison.caveats: 124-frame CFA (mono) comparison, central-overlap crop, autostretched previews (qualitative). Full-depth debayered COLOUR final = x86. Artifacts (gitignored): final_with.fit / final_without.fit / compare.png.

verdict: CLEAN / VIABLE. Smooth radial vignetting (~6% center-to-corner) + a mild left-right residual gradient; NO Milky-Way band, NO IFN clumps, NO structured nebulosity baked in (the ~1500 px drift + 373-frame median rejected the sky). A handful of faint un-rejected star specks remain. The structured cosmic dust is NOT in the flat, so dividing by it preserves the IFN.

route_decision.bias: none - matched darks carry it (CMOS); a synthetic constant offset only if a flat needs calibrating
route_decision.flat: SKY FLAT (proper = median of dark-subtracted lights) - RECOMMENDED. The with/without finals comparison PASSED (see finals_comparison: vignetting 0.44->1.06, dust preserved/revealed). Final gate = user confirms on own viewer. Fallback if rejected: vignetting-only GraXpert Division (x86).

trade_offs_and_tightening: Mild low-order L-R gradient in the flat = residual sky gradient; better handled by the separate background step - could smooth the flat to radial-only (BACKLOG).
Pure median left faint star specks; a winsorized/sigma rejection instead of median would remove them (BACKLOG).
Largest-scale IFN brightness gradient (if any) is partly in the flat's low-order component - low-risk residual, judged on finals.
Built from RAW lights (pedestal ~1007 included); a production flat would dark-subtract the lights first (x86 chain).
caveats: CFA (undebayered) - correct for the OSC pre-debayer flat order
arm base rig; the flat re-builds on x86 with the same Siril (method transfers, output not bit-identical)
